Tests that `Math.min` returns `NaN` when given an empty object as an argument.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that checks the behavior of the `Math.min` function when it receives an object as an argument. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the result of `Math.min(Object())` is `NaN`. This test ensures that the `Math.min` function correctly handles non-numeric inputs by returning `NaN`, which is the expected behavior according to the ECMAScript specification. The script provides narrow functionality focused on validating a specific aspect of the `Math.min` function.
